discovery.run_summary: Risk-off tape driven by a sharp semiconductor selloff (SMH -4.2%, NVDA -3.5%, AVGO -4%, ORCL -6.4%) alongside a VIX spike +13% and rising July Fed hike odds tied to Hormuz oil disruption. Energy (XLE +3%) leads on the Trump Hormuz toll headline; defensives (XLP, XLU, XLV, XLF) hold up. Mid-cap movers are dominated by semi-related names bleeding sympathy plus a few idiosyncratic drops without visible 8-K catalysts.

notable_index_moves: SMH -4.16% and QQQ -1.9% while SPY only -0.78% — pain concentrated in semis/AI
XLE +3% on Trump Hormuz 20% cargo toll proposal and Iran blockade restart
VIX +13% to 17 — first meaningful vol expansion in a while
XLF +0.6% into big-bank earnings week, KRE flat despite risk-off
ORCL -6.4% stands out among mega-caps, dragging AI-infra names

discoveries.name: Mercury Systems Inc
discoveries.setup: Continued multi-day slide in defense-electronics name with no visible catalyst
discoveries.thesis: Down materially again today after already dropping ~20% over five sessions, with no 8-K, no earnings scheduled, and only modest ITA weakness (-1.7%). The persistence and magnitude suggest something company-specific (contract loss, program cancellation, or leaked guidance concern), but I cannot identify it from available signals. Neither a bull (oversold bounce) nor bear (further downside) case reaches conf 3 without knowing what's driving it.
discoveries.what_confirms: A late-filed 8-K disclosing program impairment, contract loss, or restated backlog would confirm this is fundamental. A sell-side downgrade note or DoD budget-line news would similarly explain the persistence.
discoveries.what_kills: If a benign explanation surfaces (index rebalance flow, block sale, sympathy with a defense peer's guide-down), the slide loses signal value and the setup dissolves.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Multi-day 15-20% slides in defense mid-caps without visible catalysts almost always precede a disclosed negative event — worth queuing for follow-up rather than trading blind.
discoveries.catalyst: No identifiable catalyst in filings or news; ITA weakness alone doesn't explain magnitude
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals is empty {}; no 8-K, no earnings; ITA only -1.7% vs MRCY -9% today and -20% over 5 days
research_pointers: Check for any pending DoD program decisions or FY26 budget line-item changes affecting Mercury's C4I/RF business
Look for late-day 8-K filings or insider Form 4 sales in the last two weeks
Scan sell-side notes for downgrades or estimate cuts in the past week

discoveries.name: Alaska Air Group, Inc.
discoveries.setup: Pre-earnings weakness in airline compounding on oil-spike day
discoveries.thesis: ALK is down sharply into an earnings print 8 days out, on a day when XLE +3% and Trump's Hormuz toll headline drove crude higher — classic jet-fuel-cost sympathy compounded by pre-earnings de-risking. The move looks larger than what the crude tape alone justifies for a hedged carrier, and airline earnings-week fear tends to overshoot then partially retrace once numbers land, unless guidance is genuinely bad.
discoveries.what_confirms: Peer airlines (DAL, UAL, LUV) showing similar or larger moves confirms it's a sector/fuel-cost trade, not name-specific. A stable-to-lower crude tape into July 21 would support a fade.
discoveries.what_kills: If ALK is down materially more than peers, or if a specific negative pre-announcement/8-K surfaces before earnings, the sympathy read is wrong and this is company-specific.
discoveries.what_to_learn: Airlines dropping into earnings on the same day crude spikes on a geopolitical headline are a recurring overshoot pattern — the fuel-cost math rarely justifies the immediate move once hedges and pricing power are considered.
discoveries.catalyst: Oil spike on Hormuz toll headline + pre-earnings positioning (reports July 21)
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: upcoming_earnings 8 days out combined with XLE +3.03% and Trump Hormuz 20% cargo toll headline; airline is a natural short-vol target on jet-fuel fears
research_pointers: Compare ALK's move today vs DAL, UAL, LUV to isolate name-specific vs sector
Check ALK's current fuel hedge book disclosure from last 10-Q
Look at options-implied move for the July 21 print vs realized in prior 4 quarters

discoveries.name: Lattice Semiconductor
discoveries.setup: Semi sympathy selloff in line with sector move
discoveries.thesis: LSCC down ~5% on a day SMH is -4.2%, NVDA -3.5%, AVGO -4%, ONTO -5.2% — this is beta, not signal. No 8-K, no earnings pending. The magnitude is entirely appropriate to what's happening in semis today and the 5-day return is flat, meaning this is one-day tape action.
discoveries.what_confirms: N/A — classification is that no mispricing exists.
discoveries.what_kills: If LSCC continues to underperform SMH in coming sessions without a broader semi recovery, look for a name-specific catalyst.
discoveries.catalyst: Semi sector selloff (SMH -4.16%) tied to AI-trade unwind and ORCL -6.4%
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; move magnitude tracks SMH within ~1%
research_pointers: No action needed unless underperformance persists into next 2 sessions

discoveries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
discoveries.setup: Semi-equipment sympathy on broad semi selloff
discoveries.thesis: ONTO -5.2% on a day SMH is -4.2% is essentially in-line beta for a semi-cap-equipment name. Volume is actually 0.68x average, suggesting no aggressive selling — just tape following. 5-day change +0.24% means today is one-session noise, not a trend.
discoveries.what_kills: Below-average volume plus in-line beta means there's very little to trade here.
research_pointers: Skip unless a specific ONTO catalyst emerges

discoveries.name: National Beverage Corp.
discoveries.setup: Sharp single-day drop in defensive small-cap with no catalyst on a risk-off day where XLP is UP
discoveries.thesis: FIZZ down ~8% on 1.64x volume, on a day XLP is +0.55% — this is a name-specific move against the sector, not sympathy. But there's no 8-K, no earnings, and no news in the feed. Founder-controlled float with historically thin liquidity makes single-holder selling or a private analyst note plausible. Cannot articulate direction at conf 3 without more information.
discoveries.what_confirms: A late 8-K, an analyst note, or a private-placement/insider sale disclosure would explain the move.
discoveries.what_kills: If it's simply a block trade or algorithmic dislocation in a thin float, there's no thesis to have.
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown — moves against sector on above-avg volume with no visible news
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty; defensive sector +0.55% while FIZZ -8% argues against sympathy
research_pointers: Check for any FIZZ-specific news wire items (channel data, private label competition, Sparkling Ice movement)
Check Form 4s for Nick Caporella / insider activity

discoveries.name: CRISPR Therapeutics AG
discoveries.setup: Gene-editing group weakness with XBI broadly down
discoveries.thesis: CRSP -5.1% and BEAM -5.1% together on a day XBI is -2.3% is a gene-editing subgroup move roughly 2x sector beta — reasonable for high-beta clinical-stage names in a risk-off tape. Five-day drawdowns of -18% and -12% suggest ongoing sector rotation out of speculative biotech, not a single-day event. No 8-Ks.
discoveries.what_kills: A single-name clinical readout or FDA action would recategorize this as company-specific.
discoveries.catalyst: XBI -2.33% and continued rotation out of high-beta clinical biotech; VIX +13%
discoveries.catalyst_evidence: catalyst_signals empty for both CRSP and BEAM; parallel moves in gene-editing peers
research_pointers: Check for any gene-editing conference presentations or FDA/EMA regulatory news this week
Monitor whether the 5-day drawdown extends into next week

catalyst_chains.primary_event: Trump proposes 20% toll on Strait of Hormuz cargo and restarts Iran blockade — crude spikes, XLE +3%, VIX +13%

secondary_opportunities.relationship: sympathy (defense electronics on Iran escalation) — but not moving that way today, suggesting the drop is unrelated

catalyst_chains.primary_event: ORCL -6.4% and broad AI-infrastructure selloff dragging SMH -4.2%

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump pushing Senate to pass Clarity Act (crypto legislation) invoking Lindsey Graham's memory; also framing it as US-vs-China on crypto and AI
trump_signals.note: Crypto legislation passage catalyst — historically has moved COIN/MSTR sharply on regulatory clarity headlines. Not visible in tonight's mid-cap movers list but worth flagging for Michael to check crypto-adjacent names into the Senate calendar.

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump touting oil and gas prices coming down while simultaneously proposing Hormuz toll — mixed signal for energy
trump_signals.note: The rhetoric is contradictory: Hormuz toll is bullish crude, but the Truth Social post claims prices are falling. Suggests the administration may back off Hormuz enforcement if crude runs. Adds volatility, not directional signal.

ai_announcements.headline: Apple's trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
ai_announcements.summary: Apple alleges OpenAI employees improperly accessed Apple systems and pressed job candidates for Apple hardware. Legal/reputational item; no direct product overlap catalyst.

ai_announcements.headline: Altman dismisses space datacenter hype (jab at Musk)
ai_announcements.summary: Altman calls out short-term space datacenter pitches to public investors. Could be a mild negative for space-datacenter narrative names, but none of the movers in the list are direct plays.

ai_announcements.headline: Anthropic localizes Claude pricing for India
ai_announcements.summary: Anthropic introduces INR-denominated Claude subscriptions in India, its second-biggest market. Growth/pricing move, not a direct competitive product launch. No listed movers are directly exposed.

ai_announcements.headline: Waze adds Gemini-powered AI features
ai_announcements.summary: Google integrates Gemini deeper into Waze, positioning against Apple Maps. Marginal negative for Apple Maps narrative but not material; no auto/nav pure-plays in mover list.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Today's AI news flow (Apple/OpenAI lawsuit, Altman-Musk spat, Anthropic India pricing, Waze+Gemini) has no clear mechanistic link to the stocks that moved. The movers appear driven by non-AI catalysts: FIZZ (consumer staples, no AI angle), MRCY (defense, -20% on 5d suggests company-specific), CRSP/BEAM/PTCT (biotech basket weakness), ALK (airline, pre-earnings de-risking 8 days out), LSCC/ONTO (semis - possibly AI-adjacent but no specific news today tying to these announcements). No actionable AI-selloff mispricing thesis from today's announcements. Confidence 4/5 that these moves are unrelated to listed AI news.
